velmonde_cathedral_quarter.name: Aldren Pale
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.note: No relation to House Pale in Keth
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.physical: Has the specific look of someone managing the fallout of something above their pay grade.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.secret: Discovered this morning that a section of Cathedral library property records has been cleared. Does not know who cleared them. Has not yet told anyone because he is trying to determine whether he noticed something he was not supposed to notice.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.player_interaction: The cleared library records are his thread. A player who asks him directly will receive a genuinely distressed response and the beginning of a genuine investigation.

velmonde_cathedral_quarter.name: Fenwick Saltfield
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.note: No relation to the Keth House Caldren factor. Saltfield is a coastal Solvanic surname.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.physical: The specific composure of someone whose entire professional function is maintaining composure.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.disposition: Loyal to Pelvan's official interests and increasingly aware those may not be Pelvan's actual interests.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.secret: Has noticed Pelvan's Dock Quarter visits and has not recorded them in the official schedule. Does not know why Pelvan is going there. Has been deciding for two weeks whether to ask.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.player_interaction: The official access point to Prince Pelvan's schedule. His discretion about the Dock Quarter visits is discoverable and is leverage — both for the player and potentially for factions watching Pelvan.

velmonde_cathedral_quarter.name: Aldeth Halvaine
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.note: High Warden Osric Halvaine's niece — no personal relationship with the dispute
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.physical: Has the specific quality of someone who has been managing other people's money carefully enough that they trust her with it.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.disposition: Professional, precise, genuinely separate from the Church's political disputes.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.secret: Has noticed an anomaly in the Church's charitable allocation records going back twenty years — a pattern of fund movements that does not match standard charitable distribution. Has not escalated because the anomaly is old and the person it points to is senior. Is carrying this alone.
velmonde_cathedral_quarter.player_interaction: The Church's accounting manipulation — Aldeth Morne's twenty-year-old instrument — is visible in these records to someone with sufficient accounting skill. A player with Numeracy above 150 who gains access to the banking records will find what Aldeth Halvaine has been carrying.

velmonde_merchant_tier.name: Sova Halvest
velmonde_merchant_tier.physical: Careful composure, correct dress, the monitoring-station quality of a commercial presence that is smaller than the organization it represents.
velmonde_merchant_tier.disposition: Professionally correct. Has been cross-referencing manifests she does not discuss with clients.
velmonde_merchant_tier.secret: The manifests she moved to a separate drawer connect the Halverness Factors' Amber Coast operation to the Solvanic consortium funding that Sera Dawnmere found in Keth. The same thread, different end. Has been sitting on it because the Halverness Factors' home office has not instructed her on how to handle it and she does not know whether her discovery is consistent with home office intentions or contrary to them.
velmonde_merchant_tier.player_interaction: The Halverness thread — the same one from Keth — appears here. A player who has been in Keth and brings that thread here will receive the Velmonde end of it.

velmonde_merchant_tier.name: Rael Mourne
velmonde_merchant_tier.note: No relation to Aldeth Morne. Mourne is a coastal surname.
velmonde_merchant_tier.role: Salt Silence operative — Velmonde Foreign Quarter sub-section
velmonde_merchant_tier.physical: Average everything. The stall arrangement is excellent for watching the street and inconvenient for buying perfume.
velmonde_merchant_tier.disposition: Professional. Has been here for two years building a picture of Velmonde's information flows.
velmonde_merchant_tier.what_they_know: Has mapped: the Church property dispute, the Corsair payment arrangement (not the details, the existence), the Factor-General's unofficial intelligence contacts, the Halverness Factors' anomalous manifest patterns. Has not yet mapped Pelvan's Dock Quarter visits — this is a recent development.
velmonde_merchant_tier.player_interaction: The Salt Silence entry point on the Amber Coast. Approach through commercial interaction with the perfume stall. The quality of attention the player receives will be proportional to the quality of information they demonstrate they have.

velmonde_merchant_tier.name: Brenvar Duskwalker
velmonde_merchant_tier.note: No relation to the Free Fens Duskwalker
velmonde_merchant_tier.physical: Genuinely a wine merchant. Has been one for twenty-five years.
velmonde_merchant_tier.disposition: Warm, commercially astute, suspects Aldren Voss is not actually a documentary consultant but has no interest in confirming this.
velmonde_merchant_tier.secret: Voss's documentary consultancy is three offices down. Brenvar has watched the consultancy's client traffic for seven years and has noted that the client list does not match what a wine documentation specialist would serve. Has done nothing with this observation because it is not his business and the coast is peaceful.
velmonde_merchant_tier.player_interaction: Ambient intelligence about Voss through commercial community observation. Will share if asked correctly.

velmonde_dock_quarter.name: Aldren Moss
velmonde_dock_quarter.note: Third Aldren Moss in the documents — common name across Arneth. No relation to any previous.
velmonde_dock_quarter.physical: Has been organizing the Dock Quarter's informal economy for eight years. Has the specific authority of someone who is trusted by people who don't trust institutions.
velmonde_dock_quarter.disposition: Pragmatic, protective of the community, specifically aware of who is watching the district and why.
velmonde_dock_quarter.secret: Knows Pelvan has been visiting Bren Ashwick's school. Does not know what they discuss. Has been deciding for three weeks whether to tell the Factor-General, whose informal intelligence network she has been adjacent to for four years.
velmonde_dock_quarter.player_interaction: One of the most information-rich civilians in Velmonde. Her community knowledge and the Factor-General's intelligence network create a triangle that a player embedded in both can navigate. The Pelvan-Ashwick thread comes through her.

velmonde_dock_quarter.name: Torven Pale
velmonde_dock_quarter.note: No relation to any previous Pale
velmonde_dock_quarter.physical: Has been treating the Dock Quarter for fifteen years. Knows what this neighborhood's health looks like.
velmonde_dock_quarter.disposition: Direct, competent, currently receiving a patient whose symptoms he is not certain about.
velmonde_dock_quarter.secret: Examined one of the wife's patients this morning — the woman who has been asking around the Dock Quarter for a healer with specific urgency. The symptoms are early-stage. He has not filed a report because he is not certain enough to stake his professional reputation on it. Is becoming more certain by the hour.
velmonde_dock_quarter.player_interaction: The Velmonde-side thread of the disease situation. A player with medical skills who examines his patient will help confirm or rule out the Coughing Black.

velmonde_dock_quarter.name: Fen Ashwick
velmonde_dock_quarter.note: Bren Ashwick's landlord. No family relation — the surname is common in the Dock Quarter.
velmonde_dock_quarter.physical: Has been raising the rent on the rooms above his workshop for two years in small increments. Not malicious — genuinely needs the income and the school does not pay what he could get from a commercial tenant.
velmonde_dock_quarter.disposition: Pragmatic about money, vaguely uncomfortable about what the school does with it.
velmonde_dock_quarter.secret: Has received an offer from a Merchant Tier investor for the whole building — significantly above market rate. Has not told Bren. Is trying to decide between the money and the conscience problem.
velmonde_dock_quarter.player_interaction: The school's rent situation. A player who finds funding for the school's continued operation — through the Factorate, through Pelvan, through a patron — resolves this thread and earns Bren Ashwick's trust faster than any other route.

salthaven_npcs.name: Aldren Crale
salthaven_npcs.note: Third Aldren Crale — genuinely a very common name. No relation.
salthaven_npcs.physical: Has been treating Salthaven for twenty years. Currently exhausted from a different kind of effort than usual.
salthaven_npcs.disposition: Methodical, increasingly desperate. Filed a report two weeks ago and has heard nothing.
salthaven_npcs.secret: Has seven patients now. Has stopped waiting for a Factorate response and has been sending his wife to find anyone in Velmonde who might help. Has identified the likely transmission vector — a specific fresh fish supply from a boat that docked three weeks ago and distributed its catch across four households. The boat came from the southern approach and its origin before that is unclear.
salthaven_npcs.the_fish_boat: The boat that brought the infection docked once, distributed its catch, and left. Tidewatch would have a record of it passing. Salthaven's harbor records have it as a routine coastal trader. The boat's origin before Salthaven is not in the records.
salthaven_npcs.player_interaction: The most urgent NPC on the coast. Will speak to anyone with medical credentials or evident medical knowledge. His fish boat lead is the actionable thread — someone with contacts at Tidewatch or the ability to trace a vessel through harbor records can follow it.

salthaven_npcs.name: Fen Dalt
salthaven_npcs.physical: Has been processing payment lines for seven years without reading them carefully. Has the specific quality of someone who has made peace with a specific choice and does not reopen it.
salthaven_npcs.disposition: Competent at his job, has a specific blind spot that he maintains deliberately.
salthaven_npcs.secret: Knows the payment arrangement is connected to the Corsair Throne. Has never confirmed this. The non-aggression on Salthaven's fishing boats for seven years is the confirmation he has chosen to accept without naming.
salthaven_npcs.player_interaction: The harbor master arrangement is the operational heart of the Corsair payment. A player who approaches him about the specific payment line will receive deflection. A player who brings enough pressure — evidence, leverage, or a credible official request — will receive the full picture of what he processes without understanding.

salthaven_npcs.name: Aldeth Drenfield
salthaven_npcs.note: Oswin's wife. No relation to any previous Aldeth.
salthaven_npcs.physical: Has been watching her family become wealthy and uncomfortable for fifteen years with specific patience.
salthaven_npcs.disposition: Warm, perceptive, has opinions about all three of her family members' current situations that she has not shared with any of them.
salthaven_npcs.secret: Knows Torven knows something about the harbor. Knows Oswin is not asking Torven about it. Has concluded that both of them are avoiding a conversation that she is going to have to have eventually because men in her family avoid necessary conversations until someone else has them.
salthaven_npcs.player_interaction: The Hearthday family dinner access point. A player who has earned trust with any Drenfield will be at this table eventually. Aldeth Drenfield is the person at that table who will ask the questions nobody else is asking.

salthaven_npcs.name: Gorvan Fenwall
salthaven_npcs.physical: The specific authority of someone who has been trusted with community decisions for thirty years.
salthaven_npcs.disposition: Slow to judgment, accurate when he arrives at it.
salthaven_npcs.secret: Has been told about the disease situation by the Salthaven physician. Has been quietly organizing community response without waiting for the Factorate — food for sick households, quarantine of the specific fish supply chain, watching for new cases. Has not told Oswin Drenfield because he correctly assessed that Oswin would tell the Factorate and the Factorate response would be slower than what he is doing.
salthaven_npcs.player_interaction: The community response to the disease situation that is happening without Factorate involvement. A player who finds it will see both what community self-organization looks like and evidence that the situation is more advanced than the Factorate knows.

salthaven_npcs.name: Vaela Fenwall
salthaven_npcs.note: Gorvan's daughter — no relation to any previous Vaela
salthaven_npcs.physical: The specific quality of someone trusted with what a community holds most privately.
salthaven_npcs.disposition: Calm in emergencies. Salthaven's history produces this.
salthaven_npcs.secret: Has been performing the Depth faith's illness rites for the sick households. Three of the patients have described, in separate sessions, a directional awareness they cannot explain — a pull toward the sea that they describe as 'knowing which way is wrong.' Has not connected this to anything. Has recorded it in the Compact's oral records because it is unusual.
salthaven_npcs.player_interaction: The Salthaven-side thread of the unconscious effect. Three disease patients are also experiencing the directional awareness — the same phenomenon from the Keth and Thornsveld Marches artifacts. A player who encounters this description from a third geographical source has the strongest possible evidence of the cross-regional connection.

goldwater_npcs.name: Gorvan Aldeth-Morn
goldwater_npcs.note: Name confusingly similar to Senior Warden Aldeth Morne — no relation. Hyphenated family name.
goldwater_npcs.physical: A man who was wronged forty years ago and has not had a forum to say so. Carries it in his posture.
goldwater_npcs.disposition: Patient in the specific way of someone who has been patient for so long that patience has become its own kind of anger.
goldwater_npcs.secret: Does not know about the legal flaw in the reclamation instrument. Does not know the Ledger's notary has read both instruments. Has been watching the Church dispute with the attention of someone who expects to be disappointed again. Was told forty years ago that the sale was valid and the reclamation was valid and that his family had no recourse. Was told this by people with institutional authority. Has believed it because he had no reason not to.
goldwater_npcs.player_interaction: The wronged party who does not know his position has changed. A player who brings him the information about the reversionary clause — or connects him to Halvard Keld — will witness a seventy-one-year-old man discover that forty years of patient acceptance was based on incomplete information.

goldwater_npcs.name: Aldeth Greywood
goldwater_npcs.note: Fourth Greywood in the documents — common surname in agricultural Solvane
goldwater_npcs.physical: Has been filing formal complaints about Factorate taxation for fifteen years. Has the specific patience of someone who expects the complaints to be ignored and files them anyway.
goldwater_npcs.disposition: Independent, specific about Goldwater's interests, has a precisely calibrated irritation with Velmonde.
goldwater_npcs.secret: Has been approached by one of Pelvan's three advising factions — the trading house consortium — about Goldwater's potential support in a succession context. Has not responded. Has not told the Factor-General. Is waiting to understand what the approach means before doing anything.
goldwater_npcs.player_interaction: The Goldwater political independence thread. Her taxation complaints are a legitimate grievance a player can actually help with — the Factor-General has discretionary authority over certain assessment rates and a player who brings her case to him may produce a concrete result.

goldwater_npcs.name: Fenwick Dawnmere
goldwater_npcs.note: No relation to Sera Dawnmere or Vaela Dawnmere
goldwater_npcs.physical: Has the specific confidence of someone who produces something the world wants.
goldwater_npcs.disposition: Commercial, direct, specifically focused on the price suppression he believes the Velmonde trading houses have been running on Goldwater wine.
goldwater_npcs.secret: Has documented three years of coordinated low-bidding on Goldwater wine by Velmonde buyers that has cost Goldwater producers significantly. Has the documentation. Does not have access to the Guild of the Sealed Ledger's institutional machinery to do anything with it.
goldwater_npcs.player_interaction: His documentation connected to Halvard Keld's institutional access is actionable. A player who bridges the two will create a commercial justice outcome that earns significant Goldwater community trust.

tidewatch_npcs.name: Vaela Stern
tidewatch_npcs.physical: Fifteen years of approach work visible in how she reads a horizon. Economical speech, specific attention.
tidewatch_npcs.disposition: Direct. Does not maintain social pretense. Her question — 'Where did you come from before this?' — is always a real question.
tidewatch_npcs.secret: Has been explaining the coast's actual economy to Pelvan for eight months. Is the person who first described, practically and without sentiment, what the wealth concentration pattern looks like from sea level. Has not told the Tidewatch Church station about Pelvan's informal visits because the Church station's relationship with institutional power is too close.
tidewatch_npcs.player_interaction: A sailor who arrives through the southern approach will meet her during piloting. Her question and its answer determine whether she files this encounter under 'routine' or 'interesting.' A player with demonstrated knowledge of what the coast is below its administrative surface will receive her specific attention.

tidewatch_npcs.name: Aldren Keth
tidewatch_npcs.note: Fifth instance of this name across the documents — it is genuinely one of the most common names on Arneth
tidewatch_npcs.physical: Has been at Tidewatch for twenty years. The Church station knows he has outside contacts. Has never been asked to explain them.
tidewatch_npcs.disposition: Quiet, specific, the kind of person who is more useful when not defined.
tidewatch_npcs.secret: Provides advance shipping intelligence to the Salt Silence in exchange for Tidewatch's operational invisibility. The arrangement has been running for eleven years. Has noted the fish boat that brought the Salthaven infection — has the origin records. Has not connected it to the disease situation because he doesn't know about the disease situation.
tidewatch_npcs.player_interaction: The Salt Silence's Tidewatch node. Also the person who has the fish boat's origin records. A player pursuing the disease situation's source who reaches Tidewatch will find what they need — if they can access it.

tidewatch_npcs.name: Sister Vaela Morne
tidewatch_npcs.note: No relation to Senior Warden Aldeth Morne
tidewatch_npcs.physical: Has been keeping the Tidewatch station for ten years. The specific quality of someone who maintains a thing carefully without needing to own it.
tidewatch_npcs.disposition: Calm, practical, has organized her institutional obligations and her practical ones into a workable relationship.
tidewatch_npcs.secret: Knows about the Salt Silence arrangement with Aldren Keth. Has not reported it to the Cathedral because the arrangement produces accurate shipping intelligence that the Church uses for its own harbor scheduling and reporting it would end something useful.
tidewatch_npcs.player_interaction: The formal access point to Tidewatch Church resources. Her knowledge of the Salt Silence arrangement is the thread that connects Tidewatch to the broader information network.

three_faction_advisors.name: Warden Aldren Halvaine
three_faction_advisors.note: High Warden Osric Halvaine's nephew — this appointment is not coincidental
three_faction_advisors.role: Tidewarden Church political advisor to Prince Pelvan
three_faction_advisors.physical: Has the institutional confidence of someone whose appointment required only one conversation.
three_faction_advisors.disposition: Genuinely believes the Church is the coast's stabilizing institution. Is not wrong about the stabilizing. Is wrong about some of the how.
three_faction_advisors.secret: Has been providing Pelvan with accurate Church-favorable information and specifically incomplete information about the Amber Point dispute — has not mentioned the 40-year-old reclamation or the 80-year-old sale at all. Believes Pelvan's succession is in the Church's interest and is managing the information accordingly.
three_faction_advisors.player_interaction: One of the three advisors Pelvan is managing. A player who has independent information about the Church property dispute and brings it to Pelvan will demonstrate to Pelvan that his advisors are providing incomplete pictures — which Pelvan already suspects.

three_faction_advisors.name: Halvard Greywood
three_faction_advisors.note: No relation to any previous Greywood
three_faction_advisors.role: Trading house consortium representative advising Prince Pelvan
three_faction_advisors.physical: Has been negotiating commercial interests for twenty years. Carries this in how he enters a room.
three_faction_advisors.disposition: Commercially astute, genuinely believes trade prosperity is the coast's foundation, has been slowly recognizing that Pelvan is not entirely what he was told.
three_faction_advisors.secret: Has been providing Pelvan with commercial intelligence and specifically has been working to steer Pelvan toward endorsing the consortium's Goldwater price suppression arrangement. Has not yet done this because he has not found the right moment. Has noticed that Pelvan asks questions about Goldwater that suggest he already knows about the suppression.
three_faction_advisors.player_interaction: The commercial thread. His price suppression arrangement connects to Fenwick Dawnmere's documentation in Goldwater. A player who has assembled both will have the clearest picture of what the consortium is actually trying to accomplish through Pelvan.

three_faction_advisors.name: Commander Aldren Veld
three_faction_advisors.note: No relation to House Veld in Keth
three_faction_advisors.role: Solvanic military faction representative advising Prince Pelvan
three_faction_advisors.physical: The specific bearing of a military officer who has been doing civilian advisory work long enough that the bearing is the only remaining tell.
three_faction_advisors.disposition: Genuinely concerned about coastal defense vulnerabilities. Believes order is the foundation of everything else. Is the most honest of the three advisors in what he provides.
three_faction_advisors.secret: Has been providing Pelvan with accurate security assessments and has withheld one: the Coughing Black outbreak in Salthaven — which the Factorate is sitting on — has military implications for coastal garrison capacity that he flagged internally three days ago and has not raised with Pelvan because he was told by the Factor-General's office that the report was 'under review.'
three_faction_advisors.player_interaction: The military thread. His withheld garrison assessment connects the disease situation to the succession — an outbreak affects military readiness which affects the succession's security context. A player who brings him the full Salthaven picture will receive military resources and the Factor-General's attention simultaneously.
